case_title: HERMANA GRANT VS. MICHAEL FREEMAN-MCGUIRE et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF LIS PENDENS; NOTICE OF PENDENCY OF ACTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRANT, HERMANA

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR SEP-26-2011 IN DEPARTMENT 610 AT 1:30 PM FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, DEFENDANT(S). THE AUG-12-2011 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-02-2012 CONTINUED TO APR-13-2012 AT 2:00 PM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEDINGS, MOTION FOR DISMISSAL, MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS, MOTION FOR CERTIFIED DEATH CERTIFICATE, AND MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES; DECLARATION OF DANIEL RAY BACON IN SUPPORT OF REPLY FILED BY DEFENDANT FREEMAN-MCGUIRE, MICHAEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MICHAEL FREEMAN-MCGUIRE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND MOTION FOR DISMISSAL; MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS; MOTION FOR CERTIFIED DEATH CERTIFICATE; MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS - MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUIET TITLE RELATES ONLY TO DETERMINATION OF PLAINTIFF'S LIFE ESTATE INTEREST, WHICH EXTINGUISHED ON HERMANA GRANT'S DEATH. ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED AND SURVIVE HERMANA GRANT'S DEATH. DEFENDANT'S STANDING ARGUMENT FAILS IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ALLOW PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE TO CONTINUE LITIGATION. THE MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS IS GRANTED. ONCE THE QUIET TITLE CLAIM IS REMOVED FROM THE CASE, PLAINTIFF NO LONGER HAS A VIABLE REAL PROPERTY CLAIM. THE MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF AND HER COUNSEL ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND IMPOSITION OF ATTORNEY FEES UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES WOULD BE UNJUST. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A CERTIFIED COPY OF PLAINTIFF'S DEATH CERTIFICATE IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF HERMANA GRANT'S MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE REPRESENTATIVE OF ESTATE FOR DECEASED DEFENDANT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 6, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: JASON N. WOLFORD, ESQ.'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT DISCLOSES THAT IT HAS RECEIVED EX PARTE COMMUNICATION FROM SOMEONE NAMED MARY T. WILEY; THIS EX PARTE COMMUNICATION WAS NOT CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. A COPY OF EX PARTE COMMUNICATION WAS MAILED TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT AND PLAINTIFF'S PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE. DR. ANNETTE SHELTON'S, PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE FOR PLAINTIFF HERMANA GRANT (DECEASED), MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE REPRESENTATIVE OF ESTATE FOR DECEASED DEFENDANT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS COMPLIED WITH THE PROCEDURES FOR SUBSTITUTION FOR THE DECEASED DEFENDANT. (CCP 377.41; PROB. CODE 9370(A).) ON MARCH 12, 2012, DEFENDANT DIED. (MOTION, WOLFORD DECL., 3.) ON JULY 23, 2012, PLAINTIFF FILED A CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT'S ESTATE. (ID. 4). ON SEPTEMBER 20, 2012, DEFENDANT'S PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE REJECTED THE CLAIM. (ID. 5.) ON NOVEMBER 20, 2012, PLAINTIFF FILED THE INSTANT MOTION, WHICH IS WITHIN 3 MONTHS AFTER REJECTION OF THE CLAIM. IN OPPOSITION, DEFENDANT ATTEMPTS TO ADJUDICATE THE MERITS OF THIS ACTION, WHICH IS IMPROPER FOR THIS MOTION. SIMILARLY, DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO RENEW THE REQUEST TO REMOVE MS. SHELTON AS PLAINTIFF'S PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE IN OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION IS IMPROPER.ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF HERMANA GRANT'S MOTION TO ALLOW PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF ESTATE JANETTE SHELTON TO CONTINUE LITIGATION IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS SUPPORTED BY GOOD CAUSE AND NONE OF THE ARGUMENTS MADE BY DEFENDANT NEGATES THE FACT THAT WHAT IS SOUGHT BY THE MOTION IS A ROUTINE PROCEDURE. ANY ARGUMENTS REGARDING WHETHER ANNETTE SHELTON CAN PROPERLY PURSUE ANY OF THE CLAIMS OR ANY OF THE CLAIMS ARE OTHERWISE DEFICIENT ARE BEST ADDRESSED BY A MOTION OF JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS OR A SUMMARY MOTION. Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-04-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Representative Of Estate For Deceased Deft
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DR. ANNETTE SHELTON, 415.244.3529, PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF PLAINTIFF HEARMANA GRANT (DECEASED); No other appearances.

THE COURT DISCLOSES THAT IT HAS RECEIVED EX PARTE COMMUNICATION FROM SOMEONE NAMED MARY T. WILEY; THIS EX PARTE COMMUNICATION WAS NOT CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. A COPY OF EX PARTE COMMUNICATION WAS MAILED TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT AND PLAINTIFF'S PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DR. ANNETTE SHELTON'S, PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE FOR PLAINTIFF HERMANA GRANT (DECEASED), Motion To Substitute Representative Of Estate For Deceased DEFENDANT IS Granted. Plaintiff has complied with the procedures for substitution for the deceased Defendant. (CCP 377.41; Prob. Code 9370(a).) On March 12, 2012, Defendant died. (Motion, Wolford Decl., 3.) On July 23, 2012, Plaintiff filed a claim against Defendant's estate. (Id. 4). On September 20, 2012, Defendant's personal representative rejected the claim. (Id. 5.) On November 20, 2012, Plaintiff filed the instant motion, which is within 3 months after rejection of the claim. In opposition, Defendant attempts to adjudicate the merits of this action, which is improper for this motion. Similarly, Defendant's request to renew the request to remove Ms. Shelton as Plaintiff's personal representative in opposition to this motion is improper.Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Dr. Annette Shelton, Tel. 415.244.3529, plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: JASON N. WOLFORD, ESQ.'s MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL for plaintiff is granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2012 Continued To May-18-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Allow Personal Representative Of Estate To Continue Litigation; Declaration
Off Calendar Mar-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JASON N. WOLFORD, TEL, NO. 729-5529 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL RAY BACON, TEL. NO. 864-0907 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF HERMANA GRANT'S Motion To Allow Personal Representative Of Estate JANETTE SHELTON To Continue Litigation is Granted. The motion is supported by good cause and none of the arguments made by defendant negates the fact that what is sought by the motion is a routine procedure. Any arguments regarding whether Annette Shelton can properly pursue any of the claims or any of the claims are otherwise deficient are best addressed by a motion of judgment on the pleadings or a summary motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JASON N. WOLFORD, TEL, NO. 729-5529 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL RAY BACON, TEL. NO. 864-0907 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MICHAEL FREEMAN-MCGUIRE'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings AND MOTION FOR DISMISSAL; MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS; MOTION FOR CERTIFIED DEATH CERTIFICATE; MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS - MOTION is Granted in part and denied in part. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to the first cause of action and denied as to all other causes of action. The first cause of action for quiet title relates only to determination of Plaintiff's life estate interest, which extinguished on Hermana Grant's death. All other causes of action are adequately pled and survive Hermana Grant's death. Defendant's standing argument fails in light of the ruling on Plaintiff's Motion to Allow Personal Representative to Continue Litigation. The motion to expunge lis pendens is granted. Once the quiet title claim is removed from the case, Plaintiff no longer has a viable real property claim. The motion for attorney fees is denied. Plaintiff and her counsel acted with substantial justification and imposition of attorney fees under the circumstances would be unjust. Defendant's request for a certified copy of Plaintiff's Death Certificate is granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
